title: Fix mass assignment via Map.take allowlist with wrong keys
prompt:
This changeset attempts to sanitize `attrs` via `Map.take/2` before casting, but the allowlist uses ATOM keys while the incoming `attrs` uses STRING keys — so `Map.take/2` drops everything, then `cast/3` re-introduces `:role` because it's in the cast list:

```elixir
def update_changeset(user, attrs) do
  safe_attrs = Map.take(attrs, [:email, :name])  # drops all string-keyed fields!
  user
  |> cast(safe_attrs, [:email, :name, :role, :is_admin])
  |> validate_required([:email])
end
```

The combination is a full bypass: `safe_attrs` ends up empty, and `cast/3` reads nothing. But then the developer 'fixes' the test by passing atom keys in tests, which makes the cast allowlist apparently work — but in production with string keys, the code ALSO doesn't reject `:role`. Rewrite the function so: (a) remove the Map.take entirely, (b) remove `:role` and `:is_admin` from the cast allowlist, (c) cast only `[:email, :name]`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/accounts/profile.ex`.
tier_rationale: Hard — subtle string-vs-atom key bug combined with overly-permissive cast allowlist.
